core_mechanism: A perturbative renormalization-group (functional coarse-graining) expansion in the deviation from an upper critical dimension (epsilon = 4 - d, resummed at tree level plus one loop) used to compute the full size distribution and cumulants of the discrete jumps that the minimizer of a random energy landscape undergoes as a quadratic confining term is quasi-statically displaced.
brief_reason: The abstract obtains the avalanche-size distribution and its cumulants analytically 'within an epsilon=4-d expansion from a tree and 1-loop resummation, using functional renormalization,' applied to the jumps in the minimum-energy configuration of a disordered interface tied to a moving harmonic spring.
